questions.question: To use the Calendar view, what specific property must your database contain?

options.text: Status property
options.explanation: The Status property indicates the state of an item (e.g., To Do, Done), but it does not provide the date information required for a calendar view.

options.text: Name property
options.explanation: While the Name property is essential for identifying items, it does not store scheduling information needed for a calendar view.

options.text: Date property
options.explanation: Correct. The Date property stores the scheduling information (dates and times) required for the Calendar view to display items chronologically.

options.text: Tag property
options.explanation: Tags are used for categorization and filtering, but they do not contain the temporal data needed to plot items on a calendar.

questions.question: How can you create an inline calendar on an existing page?

options.text: Click + next to the database name and select Calendar
options.explanation: This creates a separate full-page calendar view for the database, not an inline calendar on the current page.

options.text: Type /Calendar view on any page and press enter
options.explanation: Correct. Typing the slash command creates a new inline calendar block directly on that page.

options.text: Drag the full-page database into the sidebar
options.explanation: Dragging a full-page database to the sidebar turns it into a sub-page, which is a different operation.

options.text: Go to Settings and toggle on Inline Calendars
options.explanation: Calendars are created per page using the slash command, not globally in the settings.

questions.question: How do you make a card span multiple days on a calendar view?

options.text: Drag the card to the right edge of the day cell
options.explanation: Dragging to the edge is the first step, but you must click and drag specifically to expand the duration.

options.text: Hover over the right or left edge, click and drag to expand
options.explanation: Correct. By hovering over the edge of the card and dragging, you can stretch the card to cover multiple days.

options.text: Double-click the card and change the Date property
options.explanation: Editing the Date property creates a new event, it does not stretch the existing card to cover multiple days visually.

options.text: Use the Property visibility settings to enable multi-day display
options.explanation: Property visibility controls which data is shown on the card face, not the visual duration of the card itself.

questions.question: If your database has multiple date properties (e.g., 'Start Date' and 'Deadline'), how do you switch which one is used for the calendar?

options.text: Go to Property visibility and select the active date property
options.explanation: Property visibility determines which properties are *shown* on the card, not which date property drives the calendar's time grid.

options.text: Open Layout settings and select 'Show calendar by'
options.explanation: Correct. The 'Show calendar by' setting in the Layout menu allows you to choose which specific date property drives the calendar view.

options.text: Drag the cards to rearrange the dates on the timeline
options.explanation: Dragging cards rearranges their position on the days they are assigned to, it does not switch the underlying property used for plotting.

options.text: Create a new database view and rename it
options.explanation: Creating a new view replicates the current database; it does not allow you to switch the underlying date property without recreating the database structure.

questions.question: How can you navigate to future months in a Notion Calendar view?

options.text: Click the back arrow (<) at the top right
options.explanation: The arrows surrounding 'Today' allow you to skip back to a past month or forward to a future month, but you must select the forward arrow (>).

options.text: Scroll down infinitely at the top left
options.explanation: Correct. Notion Calendar allows infinite scrolling down to view future months.

options.text: Click 'Show calendar as' and select 'Month'
options.explanation: This setting determines whether you see a monthly or weekly layout, not the specific date range you are viewing.

options.text: You cannot navigate to future months in Notion
options.explanation: Notion Calendar supports navigating both backwards and forwards in time.

questions.question: Which of the following describes how to convert a full-page database into an inline database?

options.text: Select 'Turn into inline' directly from the main page menu
options.explanation: The 'Turn into inline' option is only available inside the parent page's menu, not the main page menu, because it needs to become a sub-page first.

options.text: Drag the full-page database into the sidebar to make it a sub-page, then open the parent and select Turn into inline
options.explanation: Correct. You must first drag the database to the sidebar to turn it into a sub-page, then open the parent page to find the 'Turn into inline' option.

options.text: Change the database property from 'Page' to 'Inline'
options.explanation: The database type is fixed; the transformation is structural (page vs. block), not a property setting.

options.text: You cannot convert a full-page database to an inline one
options.explanation: Notion allows you to convert full-page databases to inline databases and vice versa.

questions.question: Where do you go to manually enable the calendar to start the week on Monday?

options.text: Database Layout settings
options.explanation: Database Layout settings control the weekly/monthly display mode and which date property is used, not the starting day of the week.

options.text: Language & Time in Preferences
options.explanation: Correct. You must go to Settings (sidebar) -> Preferences -> Language & Time to toggle 'Start week on Monday'.

options.text: Property visibility menu
options.explanation: The Property visibility menu is used to show or hide specific data columns on the cards.

options.text: Google Calendar integration settings
options.explanation: Integration settings are for connecting external calendars, not configuring the internal calendar start day.

questions.question: What is a limitation regarding the default date format in Notion databases?

options.text: You can set a default format for the Date property
options.explanation: Currently, there is no built-in feature to set a default date format for the Date property.

options.text: You cannot format the date property at all
options.explanation: While there is no default, you can still format dates manually in the property or via formulas.

options.text: It is not possible at the moment, though it is planned for the future
options.explanation: Correct. The help center explicitly states this is a limitation that is on their roadmap.

options.text: The format is always fixed to MM/DD/YYYY
options.explanation: The format depends on your region settings, not a fixed system default.

questions.question: How can you view a daily or yearly timescale in Notion?

options.text: Switch to the Weekly view and scroll down indefinitely
options.explanation: The Weekly view does not provide a daily timescale; it displays the week as a whole.

options.text: Use the Timeline view, which allows timescales from hours to years
options.explanation: Correct. The Timeline view is designed to show data over various time scales, from hours up to years.

options.text: Use the Calendar view on mobile
options.explanation: The mobile app has a daily view, but it does not support a yearly timescale.

options.text: There is no way to view a daily or yearly timescale
options.explanation: The Timeline view specifically solves this problem for desktop users.

questions.question: How can you embed a Google Calendar into a Notion page?

options.text: Use the Notion Calendar API to sync in real-time
options.explanation: The API is for developers building integrations, not for end-users embedding a view.

options.text: Embed from the same email address you use for Notion
options.explanation: Correct. You can embed a view-only Google Calendar into Notion if you are embedding from the same email address associated with your Notion account.

options.text: Link the Google Calendar directly from the database property
options.explanation: While you can link to a calendar, embedding requires the specific email matching feature mentioned in the article.

options.text: You cannot embed a Google Calendar into Notion
options.explanation: Notion supports embedding Google Calendars, though it is limited to view-only for the specific email match case.
